Licensed Aircraft Engineer – Base Maintenance
Job Purpose

At Base Maintenance, our expert engineers deliver unparalleled service for all aircraft checks, from comprehensive inspections to major system repairs. We handle cabin retrofits, aircraft deliveries, and detailed inspection programs for Airbus A380, A350 and Boeing 777. Our team excels in timely, cost-effective solutions for A & C checks, engine change, and landing gear changes, along with unscheduled maintenance and pre-service checks.

To carry out routine maintenance tasks and defect rectification of aircraft as per company and associated regulatory procedures.

In this role you will:
Carry out aircraft maintenance and servicing tasks in compliance with manufacturer and safety procedures. Ensure that assigned zones/aircraft are maintained as per latest revisions of approved authority Technical Publications such as Aircraft documentation, Technical Notices, Quality Notices, and Engineering Procedures Manual etc. Ensure that personal approvals/licences are valid and applicable to aircraft types.
Ensure all off-aircraft tasks i.e. spares handling, robberies, hold items etc. are handled in compliance with company procedures and that the associated paperwork is complete to ensure full transparency. Determine if tooling/equipment used is serviceable and appropriate for the task and use specialised tooling where required. Ensure that trouble shooting procedures/tools used for fault rectification are carried out in a cost effective and efficient manner.
Carry out component changes as necessary as part of trouble shooting process and/or as directed by immediate supervisor. These should be done in accordance with AMM, IPC or other manufacturer/vendor documentation to complete the task.
Ensure that all activities performed are carried out in accordance with all safety rules and regulations and in adherence to company procedures, highlighting any deficiencies/requirements (whether for aircraft or hangar use) through the effective use of reporting tools in support of the overall quality improvement process and actively participate in propagating and enhancing the Group safety culture. Ensure all work locations are kept clean and excellent housekeeping conditions are maintained.
Maintain effective communication with Colleagues including Managers and Senior Engineers to ensure a seamless flow of information and to highlight any potential operational issues. Ensure that relevant reports are related to service delays, incidents and accidents are completed in detail including the identification of issues that led to the event so that rectification can be sought through short term maintenance planning.
Manage the performance of staff using performance review process and system. Ensure SMART goal setting, interim/annual reviews and development plans are in place for assigned staff members in accordance with company procedures.
Attend training/recurrent modules as required to enhance skills and comply with CAR 145.
Qualifications
To be considered for the role you must meet the below requirements:
Licensed Aircraft Engineer TE.03

Degree/Honors (12+3) in Aeronautical, Mechanical, Avionics, Electronic, or Technical Engineering, or a Maintenance Engineering Licence.

Minimum 10 years of Engineering experience, with 5+ years as certifying staff.

Minimum of 2 aircraft type approvals with at least one of the Emirates aircraft types (A380, A350, or B777).

Preferred GCAA, UK CAA or EASA license eligible for conversion.

Fully conversant with Air Legislation and legal matters that relate to the operation and maintenance of aircraft to international standards.

Licensed Aircraft Engineer TE.02

Degree/Honors (12+3) in Aeronautical, Mechanical, Avionics, Electronic, or Technical Engineering, or a Maintenance Engineering Licence.

Minimum 8 years of Engineering experience, with 2+ years as certifying staff.

Minimum of 2 aircraft type approvals with at least one of the Emirates aircraft types (A380, A350, or B777).

Preferred GCAA, UK CAA or EASA license eligible for conversion.

Fully conversant with Air Legislation and legal matters that relate to the operation and maintenance of aircraft to international standards.

Lead a global community of Study Leaders from our Barcelona hub to deliver high quality clinical trials—faster, smarter, together. You’ll coach and enable Study Leaders across Novartis hubs, strengthen standards and ways of working, and build a trusted, resilient, high performing community. You will guide study planning and decision making, remove obstacles, and drive timely, quality, and compliant execution of Global Clinical Operations (GCO) supported studies. We’re looking for someone with significant experience leading complex global trials, proven people leadership and stakeholder management, and strength in operational planning and risk/issue management. Join us to develop talent, scale best practices, and make a measurable impact for patients.
About the Role

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Lead a community of Study Leaders, fostering collaboration, trust, and shared learning across end-to-end study activities.
Oversee the execution and delivery of a diverse clinical study portfolio within the assigned community by trial type.
Drive continuous improvement and innovation in trial operations to elevate study efficiency, quality, and outcomes.
Coach and develop Study Leaders, strengthening capabilities and therapeutic understanding.
Optimize resource allocation by assessing workload, priorities, and community needs in partnership with Study & Site Operations (SSO) leadership.
Ensure robust risk management, inspection readiness, and adherence to global standards and regulatory expectations.
Build strong cross‑functional partnerships with global teams and vendors to support seamless study execution.

KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS

Timely, efficient, and high-quality delivery of assigned studies and study-related activities within budget, and in compliance with quality standards
Proactive, iterative operational planning with effective contingencies and embedded risk management mindset in the Clinical Trial Team (CTT)
Sustainable and measurable improvements (Speed, Cost, Quality) of type of clinical trials with the assigned community
Team's satisfaction, retention and development.

ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS

Bachelor’s degree in life sciences, healthcare, or a clinically relevant discipline; an advanced degree is strongly preferred.
At least eight years of recent experience in clinical research or drug development across Phase I–IV studies.
Minimum five years of experience delivering medium to highly complex clinical studies in a global matrix environment.
At least five years of people leadership experience, ideally managing teams across multiple countries.
At least 5 years of recent contribution to and accomplishment in all aspects of conducting clinical studies of medium to highly complexity and of high priority (e.g., planning, executing, reporting, and publishing) in a global/matrix environment in pharmaceutical industry or a contract research organization, including expert knowledge of international standards International Council for Harmonization Good Clinical Practice (ICH GCP), health authorities (Food & Drug Administration / European Medicines Agency (FDA/EMA)), local/National Health Authorities regulations
Proven ability to lead virtual teams and build high‑performing, capability‑driven groups.
Demonstrated experience developing effective relationships with internal and external partners.
Strong project leadership, planning, and problem‑solving skills, with a track record of driving process improvements.

LANGUAGE

Fluent English communication skills, with strong ability to present complex information clearly.

Primary Responsibility:

The Network Support Team Manager – Europe is responsible for leading, developing, and managing the regional Network Support function, ensuring the stability, performance, and security of Americold's European network infrastructure. Building on senior network engineering expertise, this role provides technical leadership, operational governance, and strategic direction for the region’s network services. The manager will oversee support activities, mentor engineers, drive continuous improvement, and support the advancement of network strategy aligned with organizational goals.

This position requires strong hands-on senior engineering capability (aligned with the Senior Network Engineer JD), combined with team leadership, operational excellence, and strategic planning.

Main Responsibilities

Team Leadership & Management

Lead, manage, and develop the Network Support team, ensuring high-quality support delivery and continuous skills development.
Provide technical guidance and act as the final escalation point for complex network issues.
Conduct regular 1:1s, performance reviews, and development planning for team members.
Manage team scheduling, on-call rotation, workload distribution, and resource planning.
Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

Operational Management

Oversee daily network support operations across all European sites, ensuring SLA and KPI compliance.
Own the network incident, problem, and change management processes for the region.
Ensure timely resolution of all network-related service requests and escalations.
Drive operational standardization, long-term supportability, and best practices across the function.
Maintain comprehensive documentation, runbooks, and operational procedures.
Ensure all production network changes follow approved IT change control processes.

Stakeholder & Cross-Functional Engagement

Act as the primary point of contact for network-related escalations and communication within the region.
Partner with Infrastructure, Security, Operations, and Site Leadership teams to deliver stable and secure services.
Represent the Network Support function in IT governance, customer review, and leadership meetings.
Support Infosec with incident response and vulnerability remediation.

Technical Responsibilities

Building on Senior Network Engineer responsibilities:

Provide expert-level support for enterprise network technologies including:

o Palo Alto and Cisco firewalls and SD-WAN

o Cisco ISE, AAA, 802.1x

o Cisco enterprise switching and wireless

o Unifi Wi-Fi systems

o VPN technologies (S2S and RA)

o OSPF, BGP, EIGRP, and L2 technologies

Oversee proactive monitoring, capacity planning, and optimisation of the network environment.
Ensure HA, redundancy, and DR architectures are maintained, validated, and continuously improved.
Review and approve network designs, engineering work, and configuration changes.
Manage SPOF identification and remediation strategies.
Collaborate with InfoSec to ensure alignment with security standards and compliance requirements. Strategic Planning & Execution
Develop and maintain the European network strategy, ensuring alignment with global architecture and business objectives.
Shape multi-year roadmaps covering lifecycle management, technology evolution, and operational maturity.
Identify emerging technologies and assess their relevance, value, and adoption readiness.
Provide strategic input into budgeting, forecasting, and resource planning.
Lead strategic initiatives such as WAN transformation, SD-WAN adoption, authentication modernization, cloud connectivity, and network security enhancements.
Influence long-term design principles and architectural standards across the region. Project Delivery & Governance
Act as the delivery owner for network-related projects in Europe, ensuring on-time and high-quality execution.
Oversee project planning, risk management, technical validation, and stakeholder communication.
Coordinate multi-site deployments, upgrades, and expansion initiatives.
Ensure project outcomes align with defined architecture standards and support models.
Travel regularly (approx. 20%) to company sites across Europe for deployments, troubleshooting, and project delivery.

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
5+ years of hands-on senior network engineering experience (aligned with Senior Network Engineer JD).
2+ years leading technical teams, managing workloads, and providing coaching/mentorship.
Strong expertise in network security technologies including Cisco ISE, Palo Alto, Firepower, VPN, and firewall policy management.
Excellent understanding of enterprise routing, switching, wireless, and authentication technologies.
Proven experience managing network operations in a distributed, multi-site environment.
Experience contributing to or owning network strategy, technology roadmaps, or architectural standards.
CCNP (Security or Enterprise) or equivalent knowledge preferred.
ITIL certification highly desirable.
Experience with ServiceNow beneficial.
Strong analytical, organisational, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently and operate effectively under pressure.

Castellar Vidrio, perteneciente al grupo Vidrala, es una planta dedicada a la fabricación de envases de vidrio para el sector de la alimentación y bebidas. Desde Castellar del Vallès (Barcelona), contribuimos a un modelo industrial responsable y orientado a la sostenibilidad.

En el grupo Vidrala contamos con 8 fábricas en Europa y 2 en Sudamérica, donde producimos envases de vidrio y ofrecemos un amplio abanico de servicios integrales, que incluyen soluciones logísticas y actividades de llenado para algunas de las principales marcas del mercado.

Nuestro sólido recorrido industrial, nuestra presencia internacional y nuestro firme compromiso con la sostenibilidad nos consolidan como un socio estratégico en el sector de alimentación y bebidas, así como un entorno de trabajo dinámico en el que crecer profesionalmente.

En Vidrala, más de 5.000 personas elegimos el vidrio cada día. Únete a un equipo que apuesta por la innovación, la eficiencia y el bienestar de las personas.

Introducción

Como parte del área de producción de la Planta Castellar Vidrio y en dependencia de la persona responsable de Producción, la persona seleccionada deberá velar porque el proceso de moldeo de la botella sea estable, corrigiendo los defectos mediante la sustitución de material de la máquina IS.

El reto

En coordinación con la persona responsable de Producción, las principales funciones a desempeñar serán:

Efectuar cambios de todos los elementos del equipo variable tales como canalones, deflectores, moldes, punzones, boquilleras, peanas, pinzas, etc.
Garantizar las labores de engrase de material de moldeo de la máquina según la pauta marcada.
Conducir las máquinas encomendadas en tiempo de descanso de la persona responsable del/a maquinista.
Actuar circunstancialmente sobre la máquina para la corrección de defectos en base a la información recibida tanto de Zona Fría como de Zona Caliente.

Ciclo Formativo Grado Mecánica, Mecatrónica o similares.
Capacidad de respuesta, trabajo en equipo y resolución de problemas.
Deseable experiencia previa en tareas de mantenimiento mecánico.

Formar parte de un proyecto estable y de futuro, comprometido con los envases de vidrio sostenibles.

ADVANTAGE

ITIL experience.

Trabajamos para crear productos que transmitan confort, autenticidad y una forma muy nuestra de ver la vida: “La vida es intensa, ponte comod@ y disfrútala paso a paso.”

Sobre la posición que estamos buscando:

En Grupo Pikolinos estamos seleccionado una persona que se incorpore como Assistant Store Manager en nuestra tienda del Aeropuerto El Prat de Barcelona.

Como Assistant Store Manager de una tienda Pikolinos, tu misión principal será la planificación, coordinación y gestión de las actividades diarias de la tienda, además de liderar el equipo de venta.

Para conseguirlo, estas serán tus principales responsabilidades:

Gestionar de forma autónoma la superficie de venta.
Apoyar la seleccionar del equipo y realizar la coordinación, animación y formación del equipo de dependientes.
Realizar seguimientos que garanticen el cumplimiento de los objetivos marcados en cuanto a cifra de venta, políticas de precios, rentabilidad, gastos directos, fiabilidad de stocks...
Garantizar la satisfacción del cliente cumpliendo los procedimientos marcados por la empresa de atención al cliente.
Garantizar el cumplimiento de políticas marcadas por central en producto y merchandising.
Proponer campañas de comunicación en su zona.

Información sobre el horario:

El horario de apertura es de 06:00h a 21:00h y se trabaja con turnos rotativos semanales de mañana y de tarde.

Queremos conocerte si...

Tienes experiencia en un puesto similar de entre 2 y 3 años como mínimo.
Tienes capacidad para liderar un equipo de trabajo y orientarlo hacia la consecución de los kpi´s de venta.
Te puedes comunicar de forma fluida en inglés.
Quieres dejar huella y disfrutas el día a día de una tienda de moda.
Valoraremos tu experiencia en aeropuertos.

¿Qué te ofrecemos?

Un paquete de beneficios sociales, entre los que encontrarás, plan de formación inicial, desarrollo y formación en competencias específicas, servicio de psicología in Company, pares de calzado gratuitos y otros productos Pikolinos con descuento así como descuentos adicionales con nuestros partners por membresía.

¿Qué encontrarás si trabajas en Grupo Pikolinos?

Una empresa estable y consolidada, con una esencia propia que hemos conseguido a través de la experiencia en el sector calzado y la mejora continua en nuestras formas de hacer.
Formar parte de un equipo de trabajo creativo y en búsqueda constante de la excelencia.
Disfrutar de un ambiente laboral agradable, donde nuestros valores siempre se ponen de manifiesto y donde cuidamos a las personas que forman parte de nuestro equipo.
La participación en acciones de sostenibilidad a través de la ejecución de tus funciones laborales, porque para nosotros cuidar el planeta es esencial en nuestras formas de crear.

Si consideras que estos también son tus valores, ¡te estamos buscando!

Titulo: Sales Director / Director Comercial MEA (Italy based)
Descripcion_Completa:
If you have ever dreamed of leading sales in a dynamic, multicultural region within a global leading company, this is your chance! Pandora is a multinational reality present in more than 80 countries and continues to strengthen its leadership position thanks to an expansion plan with new openings every year, involving over 33,000 Pandorians worldwide.

Purpose of the role
Lead Pandora’s sales business across Turkey, Greece, United Arab Emirates, and South Africa with full P&L accountability. Unify commercial excellence with strategic leadership to deliver profitable growth and market expansion in these diverse and complex markets. Develop and execute impactful sales strategies across channels, optimize regional teams, and ensure the readiness for international mobility as part of your growth journey towards VP or General Manager roles.

Key accountabilities

Define and execute regionally customized sales strategies consistent with cluster objectives, driving revenue growth and increased market share across multiple channels (retail, wholesale, franchising).
Lead and develop high-performing multi-country sales teams, focusing on talent development, engagement, and succession planning to foster an accountable and agile culture.
Own all sales budgets, forecasts, and P&L with keen focus on profitability and strategic capital allocation.
Collaborate closely with marketing, operational, and Milan-based cluster leadership teams to align strategies and unleash synergies.
Manage relationships with key stakeholders, optimizing distribution and channel performance, including traditional retail and digital platforms.
Champion commercial transformation and digital initiatives to elevate competitive positioning and customer experience.
Ensure full compliance with local laws, corporate policies, and ethical standards.

Candidate profile

Background in Retail (Sporting Goods, Beauty, Jewelry preferred) or FMCG with proven leadership in Sales or Commercial Director roles, ideally with Country Head or Cluster Head experience and full P&L responsibility.
Strong experience managing multiple, heterogeneous markets within the MEA region or similar complex environments.
Demonstrated ability to drive sales growth, manage multi-channel commercial strategies, and lead digital transformation initiatives.
Track record of building and leading high-performing teams with a focus on engagement and talent development.
Master’s degree or MBA preferred.
Proven international exposure with cross-cultural leadership skills and a demonstrated willingness for international mobility.

Language requirements

Fluent English essential. Additional languages related to the MEA markets are a plus but not required.
Knowledge of Italian is a strong plus.

Leadership capabilities

Strategic thinker with the ability to translate insight-driven strategies into measurable business outcomes.
Results-oriented, able to deliver consistently under pressure while driving revenue, margin, and market share growth.
Skilled in developing people and fostering a high-performing, inclusive, and accountable team culture.
Adaptable change leader with strong problem-solving skills.

What we offer

Competitive total compensation package, including variable components tied to performance.
Significant opportunities for internal mobility and personal development within the regional cluster and global headquarters.
Comprehensive benefits package supporting well-being and long-term engagement.

Work location

· Milano

Mobility

Openness and readiness to international mobility are required.

Job Type: Full-time

We’ve built the foundation, fueled the engine, and mapped the route. All that’s missing is the pilot to take the wheel for Tweakwise in France and Spain.


The project : take the lead and break boundaries.

Orisha (€300M revenue) is a group that provides the means to match its ambitions. For Tweakwise, our SaaS solution for e-commerce conversion (+15%), we’ve done the groundwork: the market study is validated, the Go-To-Market plan is structured, and the prospecting tools are ready.

But make no mistake: just because the foundation is laid doesn't mean the story is written. We aren’t looking for an executor; we are looking for a Builder. Someone capable of taking ownership of this strategy, bringing their own vision, and pushing the boundaries to exceed initial goals. Your power of persuasion and your mastery of the subject will be your only judges.


Your mission : from strategy to conquest.

You will take charge of a "ready-to-go" project with the mission to make it explode :

Take ownership : you inherit the GTM plan and embody it. It’s up to you to decide how to turn these bases into concrete results in the French and Spanish markets.

Closing & Influence : you manage the full sales cycle (Mid-market & Enterprise). You convince C-level decision-makers not with a script, but with a ROI-driven vision and industry expertise.

Agility & Innovation : the plan is a baseline, not a limit. You have the freedom to disrupt the commercial approach if you identify more effective growth levers.

Growth without a ceiling : you validate the model, stabilize the markets, and build what comes next. Here, your scope will grow at the pace of your success.


Who we are ?

Founded in 2003, Orisha is a European software company supporting businesses in retail, real estate, healthcare, construction, and agri-food. With over €300M in revenue and 2,300 employees, the group provides vertical SaaS solutions that help companies manage and grow their operations.

Orisha Commerce, a brand of Orisha, provides reliable 'all-in-one' SaaS solutions for the retail industry. Tweakwise, our flagship solution for merchandising and e-commerce personalization, already helps over 400 online stores increase their conversion rates by up to 15%.


What we are looking for :

Owner Mindset : you love being in control. You know when to follow the plan and when to challenge it for better results.
Strong conviction : your credibility with e-commerce experts is your greatest asset. You sell a strategic solution, not just software.
Trilingual (Mandatory): fluent / native French, Spanish, and English are essential to navigate between your clients and our international teams.
SaaS/Tech Experience : you understand complex consultative sales and know how to navigate 2-3 month decision cycles.
This role is NOT for you if :
You want a pre-written script : we give you a strategy, but it’s up to you to invent the way to win in the field.
You don’t like "Hunting": the core of the role is acquiring new logos and closing deals.
You need micro-management : your team is international, you are the autonomous engine of your region in Barcelona.
You aren’t comfortable with uncertainty : launching a market requires resilience, creativity, and the ability to bounce back.
What we offer :

A solid foundation : you don’t start from scratch; you benefit from a European leader's expertise and a pre-studied GTM.
Zero limits : real autonomy to leave your mark and move the needle.
Motivating package : €45K - €53K Fixed + uncapped variable (your performance is your only limit).
Onboarding : full immersion at our headquarters in the Netherlands to master the product before conquering your markets.
Recruitment process :

Pre-qualification call with Clara or Séréna (French or Spanish): getting to know you and aligning expectations.
Video interview with Robert (Sales Ops Director) in French : deep dive into your sales and technical skills.
Video interview with Paul (Head of Sales) & Mark (CEO) in English : including a case study on an e-commerce site and closing.
Interview with Mar (Executive Director Iberia) or Flora (Executive Director France) : validating cultural fit and strategic vision.
Note : we conduct reference checks (with your consent) as part of the process.

Don’t wait until tomorrow, send your CV now by clicking "Apply"! #WeAreOrisha

In that time, we have supported more than 2 millions customers in France, Spain, and Italy, while investing in new verticals and positioning ourselves as a highly efficient, innovative, and competitive scale-up in a rapidly growing market.

With over 900 employees across 3 locations, we are solidifying our position as a market leader in Europe. We are always on the lookout for talent ready to join a dedicated and motivated team driven by a meaningful project. Working with us means embracing a culture of excellence, innovation, and real impact.

As Senior Business Developer for Spain, you will be responsible of:


Building strong relationships with key players in Banking / Insurance / Fintech sectors in Spain by participating in key industry events / networking activities

Closing deals & long term partnerships with the most interesting players for papernest in term of revenue potential

Managing the partners closed : develop revenues, define the roadmap, manage partner marketing initiatives, report results

Exploring new distribution channels for our offer and building new customer paths to improve our customer acquisition performance.

Your profile :

Work experience: at least 3 years of experience in a key strategic sales position (e.g. strategic partnerships, large account business development) and/or in a strategy consulting firm. A scale-up/operational experience is a plus.

Top tier business school

Skills : Excellent interpersonal skills and appetence for business development (you're not afraid to talk to the CEOs of Europe's biggest banks/real estate players), you also have strong analytical skills (you're well-versed in Excel models and ideally interested in developing new analytical skills such as SQL)

Fluent in Spanish & English

You are able to thrive in a fast-paced scale-up environment: proactive, self-motivated, driven, and organized.


What we offer :

Thrive in an international and inclusive environment: everyone has a place at papernest. With over 46 different nationalities, it’s not uncommon here to start a sentence in English and finish it en français or en español ¡


Compensation: a plan for Subscription Warrants for Company Creators (BSPCE) in accordance with company regulations, as well as a Pluxee card to manage your tax level through a voluntary compensation system across different services (transportation, dining, and childcare).

Benefits: as a home insurance provider and a supplier of green electricity and gas, we offer attractive deals to our employees. After all, there’s no reason why things should only be simpler for our customers!

Health: medical insurance through Alan or Sanitas to manage your healthcare expenses in an ultra-simple, paperless way, with up to 50% coverage by papernest (after 6 months in the company).

️ Meals & partnerships: a healthy breakfast offered every Tuesday, as well as partnerships with various services in Barcelona (restaurants, sports, leisure, and care centers).

Training: the development of our employees is essential. You will have access to ongoing training tailored to your goals, whether it involves technical, language, or managerial skills.

Career Development: numerous opportunities are available for you to grow, whether by deepening your expertise or exploring new paths. We support you in your professional ambitions.

Remote Work: enjoy 2 days of remote work per week to optimize your focus and efficiency.

Market intelligence : analyze the competition and retail trends to refine our strategic positioning.
Messaging & positioning : translate the technical roadmap into effective storytelling and create differentiating messages for each market segment.
Go-To-Market strategy : lead the launch of new modules and features (specifically regarding e-commerce and unified commerce).
Sales enablement : design sales tools (decks, demos, product sheets) and train sales teams on how to pitch the offering.

Who are you ?

You possess excellent synthesis skills to simplify complex technical topics.
You are proficient in performance analysis tools and, ideally, Salesforce, Notion, and Jira.
You are based in Barcelona.
This role is NOT for you if :

You have never worked in the SaaS software industry.
You prefer pure execution over strategic thinking and "discovery."
You are uncomfortable speaking in front of Sales teams or leading training sessions.
Working within an international organization seems too complex for you.

Initial phone screening with the Talent Acquisition team.
Interview with your future manager to dive deeper into your skills.

Final meeting with key stakeholders (Product/Sales).

Por eso, y de acuerdo con nuestra cultura People first, trabajamos para generar entornos laborales inclusivos en los que cada individuo pueda crecer y desarrollar su mejor versión. Asimismo, buscamos actuar como agentes de cambio para promover la igualdad de oportunidades en nuestro entorno, fomentando el respeto y apostando por la diversidad en todas sus formas.

Seas como seas y sientas como sientas, en Grupo Eurofirms tendrás un sitio para brillar.

Organización

Desde Eurofirms estamos en la búsqueda de un/a Business Developer que se encargue de fidelizar e incrementar la actual cartera de clientes B2B, ofreciendo los servicios de trabajo temporal.

¿Por qué trabajar con nosotros?

En Eurofirms Group creemos en el talento sin etiquetas. Apostamos por un entorno inclusivo, diverso y respetuoso, donde cada persona cuenta y puede crecer siendo auténtica.




People First no es solo nuestro lema, es nuestra forma de trabajar.

Entrarás a formar parte de la empresa de gestión de talento de capital nacional número 1 en España, con más de 30 años de trayectoria en el sector de los Recursos Humanos. Estamos presentes en varios países de Europa y Latinoamérica, y seguimos en constante expansión, poniendo a las personas en el centro.

Función

Tu misión será desarrollar negocio en Barcelona, generando nuevas oportunidades comerciales y consolidando relaciones con clientes.

Esto incluye:

Labor comercial:

Prospección activa y apertura de nuevos clientes.
Fidelización y desarrollo de cuentas activas.
Identificación de oportunidades y necesidades reales.
Desarrollo de nuevas líneas de negocio.
Coordinación con otras oficinas para garantizar un servicio excelente.

Colaboración interna:

Trabajo conjunto con el equipo de Recruiters para asegurar la calidad del servicio.
Apoyo en procesos de selección y seguimiento de trabajadores según necesidades del cliente.
Requisitos
Escucha activa, empatía y actitud positiva.
Perseverancia, iniciativa y ganas de aprender.
Capacidad de adaptación y comunicación clara.
Experiencia comercial (valorable en el sector ETT o RRHH).
Carnet de conducir.
Oferta
Apoyo continuo de tu líder y compañeros/as desde el primer día.
Plan de carrera real dentro de la compañía, con posibilidades de crecimiento y promoción interna.
Un entorno donde tu voz cuenta: fomentamos la participación, las ideas y que cada persona aporte lo mejor de sí misma.
Gestión por valores, con foco en la transparencia, el compromiso y el respeto.
Buen ambiente laboral, donde el compañerismo y la colaboración son parte de nuestro día a día.
Flexibilidad horaria real: entrada entre las 8:00 y las 10:00, salida entre las 17:00 y las 19:00.
Una tarde libre por semana, para ti.
Un día de teletrabajo semanal.
Formación continua y herramientas tecnológicas que te facilitan el trabajo.
Sistema de bonus atractivo que reconoce tu esfuerzo.
Seguro médico a los 2 años para ti y tu familia.
Benefits y descuentos en gimnasios.
La tranquilidad de formar parte de una empresa sólida, con más de 30 años de trayectoria.

To support CrowdStrike’s incredible growth, we are seeking a Sales Programs Enrichment Specialist based in our Reading, UK or Barcelona, Spain office, reporting to the Global Sales Programs Director. Identify any role-specific onboarding needs by learning and understanding the relationships between departments impacting the SDR organization.
Build out and provide coach-backs to SDR sellers to ensure an optimal ramping experience.
Equip the organization with effective resources to clearly communicate the company's positioning and unique value propositions the SDR contributes to.
Streamline & measure the success of enrichment offerings through collection of data via our feedback loop of excellence.

Minimum one-year experience in sales development or 2 years’ experience in sales enablement roles.
History of building training collateral, and job aids that resulted in sales, revenue, and/or retention growth.
Experience interpreting data to make informed recommendations.
Strong facilitation, communication, and influencing skills.
Excellent analytical, critical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Strong interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Experience in workflow/logistics management and detailed processes
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and/or Google Apps (Word/Doc, Excel/Sheets, PPoint/Slides are key tools for this role).
Experience with Salesforce, Outreach, LinkedIn Sales Navigator and Domo (preferred)

Professional who desires to invest in enriching others, high EQ, IQ and business acumen.
Team player who is equally independent
Resourceful with the ability to handle multiple tasks, and prioritize work in a dynamic, deadline-driven environment
without compromising quality.
Energetic, personable, resilient and responsive
Transparent communicator who isn’t afraid to seek out feedback to solve problems

This role will be based out of Barcelona and would require 1 to 2 days in the office/ week.

Serve as the primary point of contact for clients while collaborating with various internal stakeholders (Customer Success, Managed Services, etc.) to ensure the existing customer base is receiving the value they expect from their previous purchase

Experience of full sales cycle experience in account management or net new logo capacity, selling to a technical audience such as engineers, IT, security, and other related fields.

Titulo: ILUNION TextilCare - Jefe de producción en formación (H/M/D) Barcelona
Descripcion_Completa:
Imagina formar parte de una empresa en constante movimiento, donde las soluciones surgen de la escucha y se materializan a través del aporte de cada persona que conforma nuestro ecosistema.

En ILUNION Textil Care nuestro propósito es claro: gestionar el textil para crear valor con soluciones sostenibles. Construimos un modelo empresarial diferente que impulsa un cambio social y ambiental positivo, transformando la diversidad en inclusión, donde #TODOSSUMAMOS.

Contamos con un equipo de más de 6.400 personas distribuidas en 45 plantas industriales, la mayoría Centros Especiales de Empleo, superando el 75% de personas con discapacidad y, ofreciendo cobertura total en España, Colombia y Portugal.

En el transcurso de este crecimiento, queremos que nos acompañes como Jefe/a de Producción en formación, teniendo la oportunidad de incorporarte al equipo en Centro de Trabajo.

Marcarás la diferencia:

Buscamos una persona líder comprometida con nuestra misión de excelencia operativa e impacto social, que posea habilidades de comunicación y experiencia en colaboración interdisciplinaria. Se requiere formación en Ingeniería Industrial o áreas afines, así como pasión por impulsar proyectos de mejora continua en un entorno innovador.

Como Jefe/a de Producción en formación, serás clave en la gestión de nuestro equipo, supervisarás todas las actividades relacionadas con los procesos de producción de planta, asegurando que se cumplan los estándares de calidad, eficiencia y sostenibilidad que caracterizan a la empresa.

Más en concreto, tus retos serán:

Garantizar que los procesos productivos sean eficientes y sostenibles, contribuyendo al modelo innovador de ILUNION TextilCare y al impacto social de nuestras operaciones.

Liderar equipos diversos promoviendo un entorno inclusivo y motivador, alineado con nuestro compromiso social y ambiental.

Desarrollar estrategias que potencien el rendimiento del equipo y liderar iniciativas de mejora continua en un entorno innovador y colaborativo.

Impulsar procesos productivos sostenibles que reflejen el compromiso de ILUNION con la excelencia y la responsabilidad social.

Liderar proyectos innovadores y tener un papel clave en la transformación de la producción industrial, contribuyendo al desarrollo profesional del equipo y de la empresa.

Las ventajas de ser parte de nuestra familia:

Entorno inclusivo y diverso: Aquí, cada voz cuenta. Valoramos la diversidad y el trabajo en equipo, y fomentamos un ambiente de respeto y colaboración.

Oportunidades de desarrollo profesional: Apostamos por tu crecimiento con formación continua y oportunidades de avance dentro de la empresa.

Equipo comprometido y solidario: Formarás parte de un equipo que trabaja unido para lograr objetivos comunes, con un ambiente de trabajo cálido y cercano.

Programa Ayuda al Empleado (PAE) donde se ofrece ayuda psicológica al empleado y a sus familiares de primer grado 24 horas al día, 7 días de la semana.

Obtención de la "tarjeta +ONCE" con numerosos descuentos.

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


Practice Area


The Operations Practice (OPS) Area is one of BCG's largest functional practices. We help our clients create competitive advantage by enhancing their performance and building strategic capabilities. BCG’s expertise in Operations covers the following areas - supply chain management, procurement, manufacturing, service operations, cost efficiency, operational transformation, research & product development, program and project management, all with a strong digital and analytics overlay.


What You'll Do

As an Expert Consultant, you will be part of a BCG team and you will leverage your deep expertise on projects that reshape businesses. You will be given end-to-end responsibility for a workstream within a BCG project. You will work closely with clients to understand their issues, create strategies for change, and win buy-in for your recommendations, while also collaborating with colleagues on complex client issues. You will provide deep expertise and insight to clients and help build your expertise area within BCG. A majority of your time will be focused on supporting and executing case work.


The Expert Consulting Track (ECT) is vital to BCG’s ability to successfully meet our clients’ demand for deep expertise and advanced technical capabilities. Experts deliver next-level impact for BCG's most complex and cutting-edge client challenges. Experts focus on developing BCG's thought leadership, commercial capabilities, and intellectual property assets; and their work is essential to our delivery model. Working alongside our traditional and specialty consultants to bring the ‘best of BCG’ to our clients, Experts come from diverse backgrounds, with expertise often gained outside of BCG in industry, academia, or specialized consulting. Consulting work is varied and rigorous, much of it performed at our client sites. Projects can vary in length, size, and location, depending on the client’s challenge. Because our clients operate all over the world, you may travel internationally.


What You'll Bring

At least 5 years of work experience in top-tier consulting firms or leading industry companies
Expertise or strong interest in Procurement topics, such as: procurement transformation, category-driven value creation and procurement innovation
Strong consulting skill set with a demonstrated willingness to learn and grow: creative and hypothesis-led problem solving, framing and communicating complex ideas, thinking strategically and excellent numerical skills
Entrepreneurial, driven, and proactive mindset with a strong work ethic
Collaborative working style with a proven ability to team effectively across BCG and with clients
Ability to build trust and rapport with clients by deeply understanding their business and challenges
Strong educational background. MBA is a plus
Fluent in Spanish and English

Qui sommes-nous ?

Chez papernest, notre mission est de simplifier la vie quotidienne de nos clients en leur permettant de gérer tous leurs contrats (énergie, internet, assurance, etc.) en quelques clics. Présents dans plusieurs pays en Europe, nous sommes aujourd’hui plus de 900 collaborateurs.

Tu maîtrises l’art de la persuasion, la gestion des objections, et tu sais accompagner le client avec empathie et professionnalisme.

Français courant obligatoire.

Excellente aisance à l’oral, bonnes capacités d’écoute, sens de la persuasion, capacité à gérer des objections.

Motivé(e), résilient(e), orienté(e) résultats, prêt(e) à apprendre. Nous acceptons les débutants ambitieux.

Anglais souhaité, mais pas obligatoire pour ce poste.

Nous offrons :

Contrat CDI, temps plein, travail 100 % présentiel à Barcelone.

Rémunération : Salaire fixe de 19 624€ brut/an + prime de présence 1 380€ brut/an + primes non plafonnées.

Formation complète : produits, techniques de vente, accompagnement. Aucun besoin d’expérience préalable, le talent et la motivation sont ce qu’il compte.

Possibilité d’évolution rapide selon les performances.

Environnement de travail international, dynamique, et multiculturel.

Pourquoi ce rôle ?

Tu es intéressé(e) par la vente et la relation client, tu parles français couramment, et tu as un bon niveau d’anglais ? Tu veux travailler dans un cadre international, à Barcelone, et profiter d’un modèle de rémunération attractif basé sur les résultats ? Alors ce poste est fait pour toi.

Comment ça se passe ?

Étape 2 : Test Gorilla pour mieux comprendre ta façon de travailler, ta compréhension des consignes et certains traits de ta personnalité (45min).

Étape 3 : Entretien visio avec un recruteur pour évaluer ton profil commercial (30 min).

Étape 4 : Rencontre sur site avec un manager afin de valider l’adéquation avec l’équipe (30 min).

Titulo: Expert Consultant, Supply Chain, Operations, Spain
Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Madrid | Barcelona

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


Practice Area


The Operations Practice (OPS) Area is one of BCG's largest functional practices. We help our clients create competitive advantage by enhancing their performance and building strategic capabilities. BCG’s expertise in Operations covers the following areas - supply chain management, procurement, manufacturing, service operations, cost efficiency, operational transformation, research & product development, program and project management, all with a strong digital and analytics overlay.


What You'll Do

As an Expert Consultant, you will be part of a BCG team and you will leverage your deep expertise on projects that reshape businesses. You will be given end-to-end responsibility for a workstream within a BCG project. You will work closely with clients to understand their issues, create strategies for change, and win buy-in for your recommendations, while also collaborating with colleagues on complex client issues. You will provide deep expertise and insight to clients and help build your expertise area within BCG. A majority of your time will be focused on supporting and executing case work.


The Expert Consulting Track (ECT) is vital to BCG’s ability to successfully meet our clients’ demand for deep expertise and advanced technical capabilities. Experts deliver next-level impact for BCG's most complex and cutting-edge client challenges. Experts focus on developing BCG's thought leadership, commercial capabilities, and intellectual property assets; and their work is essential to our delivery model. Working alongside our traditional and specialty consultants to bring the ‘best of BCG’ to our clients, Experts come from diverse backgrounds, with expertise often gained outside of BCG in industry, academia, or specialized consulting. Consulting work is varied and rigorous, much of it performed at our client sites. Projects can vary in length, size, and location, depending on the client’s challenge. Because our clients operate all over the world, you may travel internationally.


What You'll Bring

At least 5 years of work experience in top-tier consulting firms or leading industry companies
Strong expertise in Supply Chain and Logistics topics, such as: digital supply chain, network optimization, inventory optimization, warehouse optimization and E2E logistics transformation
Strong consulting skill set with a demonstrated willingness to learn and grow: creative and hypothesis-led problem solving, framing and communicating complex ideas, thinking strategically and excellent numerical skills
Entrepreneurial, driven, and proactive mindset with a strong work ethic
Collaborative working style with a proven ability to team effectively across BCG and with clients
Ability to build trust and rapport with clients by deeply understanding their business and challenges
Strong educational background. MBA is a plus
Fluent in Spanish and English


Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

Presentación

En Claire Joster creemos en el talento único de cada persona y sabemos que la diversidad aporta valor a los equipos, impulsando organizaciones más innovadoras, creativas y eficientes. Por eso, como parte de Eurofirms Group, y de acuerdo con nuestra cultura People first, trabajamos para generar entornos laborales inclusivos en los que cada individuo pueda crecer y desarrollar su mejor versión. Asimismo, buscamos actuar como agentes de cambio para promover la igualdad de oportunidades en nuestro entorno, fomentando el respeto y apostando por la diversidad en todas sus formas.

Seas como seas y sientas como sientas, en Claire Joster tendrás un sitio para brillar.

Organización

Nuestro cliente es una conocida entidad pública ubicada en Barcelona

Función

Tus principales responsabilidades incluirán:

Contribuir al análisis y evaluación de la adecuación y efectividad de los controles internos del grupo, identificando riesgos y oportunidades de mejora.
Colaborar en el diseño, desarrollo e implantación de nuevas metodologías, procesos y herramientas para las funciones de fiscalización y auditoría interna.
Participar en la planificación anual de los planes de control mediante el análisis de riesgos y la definición de procedimientos y pruebas.
Ejecutar pruebas de auditoría para verificar la eficacia y eficiencia de los controles establecidos en procesos clave.
Dar soporte en la coordinación y supervisión de trabajos de fiscalización y auditoría interna realizados con medios externos.
Revisar procesos de contratación administrativa, asegurando el cumplimiento de la normativa vigente y la correcta definición de convenios.
Analizar la solvencia económica en procedimientos de contratación.
Evaluar el cumplimiento normativo y la viabilidad de los convenios formalizados por las empresas del grupo.
Redactar informes y presentaciones de resultados que recojan hallazgos, conclusiones y recomendaciones.
Asesorar a otras áreas en materia de control interno y cumplimiento normativo.
Monitorizar la implementación de recomendaciones derivadas de auditorías previas, garantizando acciones correctoras adecuadas.
Colaborar con otros departamentos y organismos de control para promover la transparencia y la rendición de cuentas.
Participar en el tratamiento documental requerido por órganos de control externo.
Velar por el cumplimiento de las medidas de prevención y seguridad laboral.
Desempeñar otras tareas asignadas relacionadas con su capacitación y experiencia.
Requisitos

Requisitos:

Titulación universitaria en ADE, Economía, Finanzas, Derecho o similar.
Se valorará máster o posgrado en auditoría de cuentas y/o auditoría pública.
Se valorará certificación en auditoría interna o auditoría de cuentas.
Experiencia mínima de 3 años en fiscalización, auditoría interna o firma de auditoría realizando funciones similares.
Se valorará experiencia en el sector público.
Conocimientos avanzados de herramientas ofimáticas en entorno Windows.
Se valorará experiencia con SAP.
Nivel alto de catalán y castellano, hablado y escrito.
Disponibilidad y flexibilidad horaria.
Carnet de conducir B1.
Oferta

Se ofrece:

Contrato de sustitución con posterior paso a indefinido.
Horario de 37,5 horas semanales, con flexibilidad de entrada entre las 7h y las 9h, y jornada intensiva.
1 día de teletrabajo a la semana.
37 días laborables de vacaciones.

About the Role:
We are seeking an experienced Cyber Security Technical Trainer to drive the technical and commercial proficiency of our global sales and solutions engineering partners. In this role, you will serve as an architect of partner success, developing and delivering expert instruction on the CrowdStrike platform. A main focus is to train the trainers at our distributors.

Success in this position demands a multidisciplinary blend of technical cybersecurity expertise, proficiency in IT infrastructure, comfort with Agentic AI and AI for Security and educational leadership, paired with understanding of sales motions. You will act as a force multiplier, enabling partner Account Executives (AEs) and Sales Engineers (SEs) to articulate the business and technical value of CrowdStrike's portfolio to deliver the right solutions to customers and prospects while staying at the cutting edge of industry trends.

What You'll Do:
Deliver high-impact instructor-led training (ILT)—both virtual and in-person—spanning the entire sales lifecycle—from initial opportunity identification and value positioning to the delivery of world-class "outcome-based" technical demonstrations, health checks, and risk reviews, and Proof of Value (POV) engagements.
Enable AEs and SEs to articulate the intersection of business value and technical superiority, ensuring they deliver the right solutions to complex prospect environments.
Leverage deep expertise in IT infrastructure, including data centers, cloud, SaaS and networking, to provide context-rich training.
Execute Train-the-Trainer (T3) instruction to certify partners, providing ongoing mentorship to ensure global instructional quality.
Maintain subject matter technical and commercial expertise across the CrowdStrike portfolio and deep understanding of the competitive landscape.
Maintain a rigorous continuous learning cadence, staying current on the evolving threat landscape and industry trends through consistent technical and commercial upskilling, ensuring "lead-from-the-front" subject matter expertise with hands-on experience in Falcon via personal learning and shared labs.
Demonstrate the technical agility to master and deliver training on CrowdStrike's Agentic AI solutions across diverse use cases.
Monitor training performance metrics to provide strategic recommendations for content enhancement and instructional design.
Leverage data-driven insights to evaluate the impact of enablement programs and track participant mastery, ensuring learning outcomes translate directly into field performance.
Evaluate the readiness of prospective trainers, ensuring a consistent, world-class enablement experience across the global partner network.
Act as an evangelist for new products, solutions, and initiatives, fostering internal momentum while leveraging performance metrics to optimize curriculum impact and ensure global partner readiness.
Collaborate with instructional designers to enhance existing content and develop new courses by providing subject matter expertise and real-world implementation feedback.
Collaborate closely with our Alliances and Solution Architecture teams, to effectively prioritize and focus on key partner initiatives and regional plans
Act as a cross-functional bridge between the field and CrowdStrike internal teams to ensure that enablement assets, product direction, and marketing strategies align with the evolving commercial and technical requirements and competitive challenges encountered by our global partners.
What You'll Need:
10+ years experience in presales engineering, technical training, or consulting within the cybersecurity industry.
Practical, hands-on expertise in one or more of the following enterprise cybersecurity areas: SOC operations, SIEM/SOAR, EDR/XDR, Threat Intelligence, Identity Security, SaaS Security, Cloud Security.
Hands-on proficiency in IT infrastructure and architecture, including cloud and on-premises networking and compute platforms.
Delivers clear, impactful presentations and interactive training to both technical and business audiences.
Exhibits an exceptional ability to demystify complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders while maintaining credibility with engineering teams.
Possesses working knowledge of MEDDIC and value-based selling methodologies.
Holds a bachelor’s degree or higher in a technical or business-relevant field.
Demonstrates strong intellectual curiosity and competitive drive, with a clear commitment to continuous learning and professional growth.
Willing to travel as required to support partner and customer events.
Fluent in English.
Bonus Points:
Hands-on experience in cybersecurity operations.
CrowdStrike Falcon certifications (e.g., CCFA, CCFR) or industry-recognized certifications (e.g., SANS, CISSP, CEH, OSCP).
Strong hands-on understanding of the CrowdStrike Falcon platform or equivalent competitive solutions across endpoint security, threat intelligence, or SIEM integration.
Experience across incident response, digital forensics, or security-focused AI infrastructure.
Background in curriculum development or in designing and delivering T3 technical training programs at scale.

Escalating issues

Job Description
What you can expect

En Aduanas Alie (empresa del grupo Rhenus Logistics) buscamos incorporar un/a Warehouse Manager en nuestra delegación de Polinyà. La persona seleccionada será responsable de la gestión eficiente del almacén, asegurando que todas las actividades de recepción, almacenamiento, y distribución se realicen de manera segura y eficiente, garantizando un servicio de alta calidad.

Principales Tareas:
Gestionar las operaciones diarias del almacén.
Coordinar la recepción, almacenamiento y envío de mercancías.
Supervisar y dirigir al equipo de almacén, asignando tareas y asegurando el cumplimiento de objetivos.
Implementar y mantener procesos operativos eficientes.
Gestionar inventarios, asegurando la precisión y disponibilidad de productos.
Garantizar el cumplimiento de las normativas de seguridad y calidad.
Optimizar el espacio y los recursos del almacén.
Realizar reportes periódicos de desempeño y KPIs.
Colaborar con otros departamentos (transporte, compras, etc.).
What you bring
Requisitos:
Gradouniversitario en Logística, Administración o campo relacionado (valorado).
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en gestión de almacenes o logística.
Conocimientos de software de gestión de almacenes y Excel avanzado.
Capacidad demostrada de liderazgo y gestión de equipos.
Habilidades organizativas y de resolución de problemas.
Conocimiento de normativas de seguridad y certificaciones OEA.
Carnet de carretillero (valorado).
Se valorará estar en posesión del certificado de discapacidad igual o superior al 33%.

RHENUS ROAD SPAIN es un empleador que ofrece igualdad de oportunidades. Todas las personas tendrán la oportunidad de ser consideradas para el empleo sin importar su raza, color, religión, origen nacional, ascendencia, ciudadanía, edad, sexo, género, identidad de género, expresión de género, orientación sexual, estado civil, discapacidad, embarazo, parto y condiciones médicas relacionadas, o cualquier otra característica protegida por las leyes aplicables. Rhenus se esforzará por realizar una adaptación razonable a las limitaciones físicas o mentales conocidas de un solicitante calificado con una discapacidad, a menos que la adaptación imponga una dificultad excesiva en el funcionamiento de nuestro negocio.

Business Unit
Road Freight
Logistics needs intelligent management so that goods are in the right place at the right time -our transport solutions guarantee exactly that. Rhenus Road Freight's product portfolio ranges from groupage freight, partial and full loads to hazardous goods, and special transports. Together with accompanying services such as insurance or customs, this results in individual and industry-specific full-service packages. We are pragmatic and uncomplicated. At the same time, our permanent course for growth opens up long-term perspectives for all our employees, whether they are specialists or career changers.

Presentación

Somos la firma global de talento: Selección, headhunting, formación y consultoría de Eurofirms Group.

En Claire Joster creemos en el talento único de cada persona y sabemos que la diversidad aporta valor a los equipos, impulsando organizaciones más innovadoras, creativas y eficientes. Por eso, como parte de Eurofirms Group, y de acuerdo con nuestra cultura People first, trabajamos para generar entornos laborales inclusivos en los que cada individuo pueda crecer y desarrollar su mejor versión.

Asimismo, buscamos actuar como agentes de cambio para promover la igualdad de oportunidades en nuestro entorno, fomentando el respeto y apostando por la diversidad en todas sus formas. Seas como seas y sientas como sientas, en Claire Joster tendrás un sitio para brillar.

Organización

Nos encontramos en la búsqueda de un/a Talent Acquisition Specialist (Cliente Final) para una importante Multinacional del sector logístico (Ubicación Baix Llobregat).

Función

En dependencia directa del CEO y la Manager del departamento de RRHH, te responsabilizarás de:

Gestión íntegra de los procesos de selección para los distintos Departamentos Corporativos en Headquarters y en las filiales de la Compañía (desde la definición del puesto hasta la contratación, onboarding y acogida).
Gestión de la incorporación: oferta, negociación de condiciones laborales, gestión documental e incorporación.
Onboarding de nuevos empleados: ruta por las instalaciones, formación inicial y presentación de empresa.
Participación en la estrategia de Selección y Captación de Talento, contacto directo con los Departamentos y Delegaciones para el análisis de necesidades y seguimiento de los procesos y candidaturas.
Análisis del mercado laboral, movimientos de contratación en sectores y asesoría en esta materia al cliente interno.
Contacto con Universidades, centros y organismos de formación para el establecimiento de colaboraciones, así como participación en foros de empleo y eventos de talento.
Gestión y análisis de KPIs de rotación, altas, bajas y movimientos de trabajadores para el liderazgo de la estrategia de crecimiento de la empresa en materia de plantilla.
Desarrollo de proyectos en colaboración con Departamentos afines como Marketing, Legal, Administración u otros para la consecución de objetivos en estrategias del negocio.

Grado Universitario y/o Licenciatura en Psicología, Sociología, Relaciones Laborales, ADE o similares.
Experiencia requerida de 3-4 años desempeñando funciones similares.
Alto nivel de interlocución, proactividad, capacidad analítica y de trabajo en equipo
Enfocado a objetivos, habituado a cumplir con deadlines
Requerido nivel medio de inglés.
Oferta
Participación en proyectos innovadores y de gran interés profesional, con una de las empresas líderes del sector.
Integración en equipos altamente cualificados y en constante evolución.
Un entorno de trabajo colaborativo, dinámico y de crecimiento continuo.
Contrato indefinido con altas posibilidades de evolución y crecimiento profesional dentro de una empresa global.

Salario competitivo, con progresión y desarrollo.
Plan de retribución flexible.

Formarás parte de un equipo en continua evolución, altamente motivado y con una clara vocación de servicio. Participarás en la construcción y gestión de los roles corporativos en el área fiscal en un grupo multinacional con presencia en más de 20 países.

Descripción de la empresa


Sports Direct.com es el éxito del sector retail de la última década. Nuestro inigualable crecimiento en el Reino Unido y Europa nos hace destacar del resto.

No tenemos intención de reducir el ritmo y contamos con un extraordinario plan de continua expansión.

Parte de nuestro éxito radica en nuestros gerentes dinámicos, dedicados y orientados a las ventas. Y, a medida que el crecimiento genera nuevas oportunidades, buscamos un supervisor con experiencia para liderar nuestro equipo


Descripción del empleo


En Sports Direct necesitamos que nuestros líderes se desvivan por los valores de nuestra empresa

Think without limits - Piense rápido, sin miedo, y consiga que su equipo le siga.

Own it and back yourself - Esfuércese por lo más básico, su función y los resultados

Be relevant - Relevante para su personal, nuestros socios y el planeta.

Queremos que aportes tus habilidades y pasión por el comercio minorista para seguir mejorando la forma en que servimos a nuestros clientes; lo harás posible ejerciendo un impacto positivo en los siguientes ámbitos:

Liderazgo y gestión de equipos

Liderar un equipo reducido en uno de nuestros departamentos de la tienda
Ser un modelo a seguir en lo que se refiere al suministro de una experiencia al cliente de primera clase e instruir al equipo para que sea capaz de proporcionar continuamente ese tipo de experiencia
Crear un ambiente de trabajo agradable para el personal
Desarrollar el mejor equipo posible mediante la planificación de estrategias de sucesión
Proporcionar feedback inmediato y felicitar los logros

Visual Merchandising

Liderarás al equipo en la correcta presentación de los productos de la tienda y pondrás en práctica estándares de merchandising visual que estén en consonancia con la marca Sports Direct.

Ventas y operaciones

Serás fundamental para lograr resultados y superar los objetivos establecidos, impulsando el rendimiento de la tienda para alcanzar su máximo potencial.
Liderar al equipo para garantizar su eficacia a la hora de implementar siempre las mejores prácticas a fin de contribuir al cumplimiento de los objetivos comerciales y al reconocimiento de la marca.

Tu desarrollo

Estamos comprometidos con el desarrollo de nuestro personal y, para ayudarte a alcanzar tus objetivos personales y comerciales, te brindaremos apoyo mediante nuestros recursos de desarrollo del personal. El personal recibe apoyo continuo a través de nuestro departamento de aprendizaje y desarrollo, desde el cual ofrecemos una variedad de oportunidades de avance profesional, comenzando por nuestra introducción a Frasers y prosiguiendo con una serie de cursos que permiten un desarrollo constante.


Requisitos

Experiencia reciente en supervisión
Pasión por el sector retail y las personas
Dotes de líder seguro de sí mismo capaz de motivar y plantear retos
Basado en los datos y orientado a los objetivos
Detallista
Capacidad para comunicarse a todos los niveles
Deseo y ambición por ser el/la mejor
Que promueva los valores de Sports Direct y nuestra cultura tanto dentro como fuera de la empresa

Bonificaciones específicas en una gran variedad de productos

Descripcion_Completa:
Meet your Recruiter!
Maria Rocha

Languages:
English
Portuguese

maria@babelprofiles.com

Our client is a global leader in home and building automation. We are looking for an experienced Head of Accounting to lead the Iberia finance operations, covering Spain and Portugal. This role offers the opportunity to stabilize and transform the finance function following a regional reorganization. You will manage a talented team of 7 professionals and ensure compliance with local and IFRS standards, delivering accurate reporting, robust internal controls, and strategic financial insights.

You will focus on process optimization and transformation management across several projects planned for 2026 within the Finance department. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a key role in a dynamic and innovative organization while contributing to strategic business goals!

We are committed to building an inclusive workplace. All qualified candidates will be considered for the role regardless of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, or any other protected status, in accordance with local laws.

Languages:
Full professional level of Spanish
Professional level of English
Catalan & Portuguese are a plus
Barcelona
Salary: Competitive salary
Industry: Technology
Contract: Permanent
Main Responsibilities

As a Head of Accounting, you will be a key partner in driving financial excellence across Iberia. You will:

Team Leadership: Manage and develop the finance team, assigning tasks, providing support, and ensuring high performance
Finance Transformation: Lead and support the integration of two companies into SAP, ensuring smooth coordination with controlling and reporting teams. Drive process automation, and financial reporting improvements
Accounting Oversight: Oversee local and IFRS accounting, ensuring timely and accurate monthly, half-yearly, and annual closings.
Compliance & Control: Implement and monitor internal controls, ensuring regulatory and tax compliance across markets
Audit Management: Oversee internal and external audits, ensuring full compliance with corporate and legal requirements
Strategic Finance Leadership: Provide financial insights and recommendations to senior management, supporting mergers, acquisitions, and business decisions
Core Skills/Experience
Advanced degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field; professional accreditation (ACA, ACCA) preferred
Experience: +8 years in finance/accounting, with at least 4 years in a leadership role
Leadership & Management: Proven experience in leading finance teams and managing diverse stakeholder
Technical Expertise: Strong knowledge of IFRS, local taxation, and compliance requirements
Finance Transformation: Experience with SAP integration, process automation, and financial system enhancements
Analytical & Strategic Thinking: Ability to align financial processes with business objectives and drive efficiency
Communication & Influence: Strong stakeholder management skills and ability to work cross-functionally
Detail-oriented, with a hands-on and organized approach

Competitive salary + bonus
A dynamic, flexible work environment in a company that values innovation and professional growth
Be part of an industry-leading global organization that pioneers automation solutions

Step 3: Online interview with the VP of Finance
Step 3: F2F interview with the HR team

Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Madrid | Barcelona

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X


We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
You'll be part of our rapidly growing team who will have a chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.
Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, creative, and communicative.
We are looking for dedicated individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and redefining organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following:
Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
Significant experience applying advanced analytics to business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
Have strong project management skills


#BCGXjob


What You'll Bring

REQUIREMENTS:
Currently enrolled in a university master’s or PhD degree program in Computer Research Science, Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research, or related field.
You must be in your last academic year (Master Degree)
You need full-time availability for at least 8 weeks to have a complete immersion in a project
You need a fluent level of Spanish, both written and spoken, in order to serve our clients (also fluent English)


TECHNOLOGIES:
Programming Languages: Python

SOBRE NOSOTROS

Una gran familia donde crecer y alcanzar el éxito

Somos el fabricante francés de equipamiento para el hogar líder en el sector, con una red de más de 500 tiendas en todo el mundo. Esto es posible gracias a que todos los que hacemos Schmidt cada día compartimos una misma pasión, crear soluciones reales y de calidad.

Trabajar en Schmidt no significa solo vender mobiliario. Desde cualquiera de nuestras tiendas, marcarás la diferencia en la vida de nuestros clientes, creando vínculos estrechos y desarrollando tu carrera junto a grandes profesionales. Para ello contarás con cursos formativos y oportunidades de movilidad y crecimiento dentro de la compañía.




Formar parte del equipo de diseño y ventas significa hacer realidad los sueños de nuestros clientes.

En Schmidt, tu cometido será diseñar y vender proyectos hechos a medida, ofreciendo un servicio personalizado y haciendo felices a tus clientes.


¿CÓMO ES SER VENDEDOR/A EN SCHMIDT?

Ser Vendedor/a-Diseñador/a en Schmidt es un puesto muy dinámico, no solo te dedicarás al ámbito de ventas, sino también diseño, gestión de clientes y gestión de proyectos. Manejarás tus proyectos de principio a fin.

Darás la bienvenida a tus clientes, escucharás sus necesidades con atención y serás capaz de proponerles ideas creativas. Desde la primera vez que acudan a la tienda, construirás con el cliente el proyecto que mejor se adapte a sus necesidades.


¿A QUIÉN BUSCAMOS?

Como Vendedor/a-Diseñador/a, necesitarás ser un/a buen/a administrador/a de proyectos, apasionado por el trato al cliente, con capacidad de escucha y siempre dispuesto a ofrecer soluciones creativas y personalizadas.

Buscamos a una persona creativa que esté tan entusiasmada como el propio cliente para transformar su hogar.

La persona indicada para este puesto contará con:

Experiencia demostrable como vendedor/a de mínimo 2 años, preferiblemente en venta de mobiliario.
Perfil comercial y dinámico. Persona acostumbrada a trabajar por objetivos de lunes a sábado o en los horarios comerciales de la zona ofertada.
Informática a nivel usuario.
Permiso de conducción.
Vehículo propio.
Nivel estudios mínimos Bachillerato o similar. Se valorarán idiomas (inglés, francés).
Indispensable residencia en la provincia de la oferta.
